things.name: Sinsheim Auto & Technik Museum
things.description: Europe's most spectacular private technology museum with Concorde, Tupolev Tu-144 and 3000 exhibits including F1 cars and rare aircraft

things.name: PreZero Arena
things.description: State-of-the-art stadium of TSG Hoffenheim, located next to the Sinsheim museum and renowned for its distinctive design

things.name: Burg Steinsberg
things.description: Dramatic medieval hilltop castle ruin nicknamed the compass of the Kraichgau, with panoramic views over the Rhine plain

things.name: Heidelberg Old Town
things.description: Germany's most romantic university town with a spectacular ruined castle above the Neckar River, just 20 minutes by train

things.name: Sinsheim City Centre
things.description: Charming small-town centre with a historic market square, half-timbered buildings and weekly produce markets

things.name: Kraichgau Landscape
things.description: Rolling hills and vineyards of the Kraichgau between Karlsruhe and Heidelberg, excellent for cycling and hiking tours

things.name: Elsenz River Walk
things.description: Peaceful riverside trail along the Elsenz through Sinsheim offering an easy introduction to the Kraichgau countryside

things.name: Mannheim Palace
things.description: One of the largest baroque palaces in Europe, now housing Mannheim University, easily reached by train from Sinsheim

things.name: Speyer Cathedral
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage Romanesque imperial cathedral of 1061 with the largest crypt in Germany, reachable from Sinsheim

things.name: Sinsheim Stadtmuseum
things.description: Local history museum documenting Sinsheim's evolution from a medieval market town to home of the famous technology museum
